name: Liepard

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

name: Scraggy
flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Ruffians Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Darkness Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

name: Trubbish
flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.text: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

name: Watchog

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Hyper Fang attack's base damage is 240.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

name: Herdier

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

name: Bouffalant ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bouffer
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Mandibuzz

abilities.name: Look for Prey
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less that you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

flavorText: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

name: Ferroseed
flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Special Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

name: Keldeo ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Vullaby
flavorText: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

name: Ferrothorn

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

name: Reshiram ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Hydreigon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Greedy Eater
abilities.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Dark Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

name: Patrat

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

name: Whimsicott ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Jellicent ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Oceanic Curse
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

name: Whimsicott ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

name: Harlequin
rules: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Hilda
rules: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Keldeo ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Reshiram ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Hydreigon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Greedy Eater
abilities.text: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Dark Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Jellicent ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Oceanic Curse
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Bouffalant ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bouffer
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Hilda
rules: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Zekrom ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Voltage Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Reshiram ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Snivy
flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

name: Servine

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

name: Serperior ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Regal Cheer
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Pokémon do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Command the Grass
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

name: Simisage
flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Bemusing Aroma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Lively Needles
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

name: Karrablast

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Shelmet in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

name: Victini

attacks.name: V-Force
attacks.text: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Dangerous Reaction
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

name: Darumaka
flavorText: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Smashing Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

name: Volcarona

abilities.name: Torrid Scales
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

name: Tirtouga

attacks.name: Ancient Seaweed
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Item card in your opponent's discard pile.

flavorText: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Snotted Up
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

name: Panpour

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

name: Carracosta

abilities.name: Mighty Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

name: Simipour
flavorText: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

name: Alomomola

abilities.name: Gentle Fin
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The energy made in its cheeks' electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

name: Kyurem ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burst
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Buzz Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

name: Munna

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Dream Calling
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Fennel cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Cellular Ascension
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Evo-Lariat
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.

flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

name: Solosis
flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

name: Duosion

attacks.name: Cellular Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Thundurus

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Disaster Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

name: Zekrom ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Voltage Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Slight Shift
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

name: Drilbur
flavorText: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

name: Excadrill ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Piercing Drill
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

name: Meloetta ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Debut Performance
abilities.text: If you go first, this Pokémon can use attacks during your first turn.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Timburr
flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

name: Crustle

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

name: Conkeldurr

abilities.name: Craftsmanship
abilities.text: This Pokémon gets +40 HP for each Fighting Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

name: Throh

attacks.name: Shoulder Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

name: Venipede

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Cursed Slug
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Wild Lances
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

name: Scolipede

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand.

flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Gear Coating
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have any Metal Energy attached take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

name: Genesect ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Metallic Signal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Metal Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Axew

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Axe Blast
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance separating them.

name: Fraxure

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they've been fighting.

name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 4 cards.

attacks.name: Swift Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Righteous Edge
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

name: Fennel
rules: Heal 40 damage from each of your Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Braviary

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

name: N's Plan
rules: Move up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Energy Coin
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, search your deck for a Basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

name: Air Balloon
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

name: Audino

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

name: Rufflet
flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

name: Antique Cover Fossil
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

abilities.name: Protective Cover
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

name: Pokégear 3.0
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Professor's Research
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Prism Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to a Basic Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Bemusing Aroma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Buzz Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Dream Calling
attacks.text: You may search your deck for any number of Fennel cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

name: Simisage
flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Lively Needles
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Dangerous Reaction
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

name: Simipour
flavorText: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

name: Servine

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

name: Darumaka
flavorText: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

name: Panpour

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

name: Alomomola

abilities.name: Gentle Fin
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

name: Emolga

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The energy made in its cheeks' electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

name: Eelektrik

abilities.name: Dynamotor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

name: Snivy
flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

name: Karrablast

abilities.name: Stimulated Evolution
abilities.text: If you have Shelmet in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Smashing Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

name: Volcarona

abilities.name: Torrid Scales
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Snotted Up
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

name: Tirtouga

attacks.name: Ancient Seaweed
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Item card in your opponent's discard pile.

flavorText: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

name: Carracosta

abilities.name: Mighty Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

name: Munna

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

name: Solosis
flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

name: Duosion

attacks.name: Cellular Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Slight Shift
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

name: Drilbur
flavorText: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

name: Timburr
flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

name: Conkeldurr

abilities.name: Craftsmanship
abilities.text: This Pokémon gets +40 HP for each Fighting Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

name: Throh

attacks.name: Shoulder Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

name: Crustle

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

name: Venipede

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

name: Scolipede

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand.

flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Wild Lances
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

name: Serperior ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Regal Cheer
abilities.text: Attacks used by your Pokémon do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Command the Grass
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Tighten Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Cursed Slug
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Righteous Edge
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Axe Blast
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance separating them.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Gear Coating
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have any Metal Energy attached take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Fraxure

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they've been fighting.

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.text: Draw 4 cards.

attacks.name: Swift Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

name: Braviary

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

name: Kyurem ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burst
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Klang

attacks.name: Hard Gears
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

name: Audino

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Debut Performance
abilities.text: If you go first, this Pokémon can use attacks during your first turn.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Zekrom ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Voltage Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Excadrill ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Piercing Drill
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Zekrom ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Voltage Burst

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Debut Performance
abilities.text: If you go first, this Pokémon can use attacks during your first turn.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Excadrill ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Piercing Drill

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: N's Plan
rules: Move up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Victini

attacks.name: V-Force
attacks.text: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Victini

attacks.name: V-Force
attacks.text: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Mega Venusaur ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solar Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic Grass Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jungle Dump
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stomping Wood
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jam-Packed
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Grass Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pumped-Up Whip
