name: Lillie's Determination
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Lt. Surge's Bargain
rules: Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mega Signal
rules: Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Mystery Garden
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Night Stretcher
rules: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Mega Absol ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Terminal Period
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 6 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Claw of Darkness
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard a card you find there.

name: Lillie's Determination
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mega Gardevoir ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overflowing Wishes
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mega Symphonia
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Mega Latias ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Illusory Impulse
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Wally's Compassion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Premium Power Pro
rules: During this turn, attacks used by your Fighting Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Wally's Compassion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mega Venusaur ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solar Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic Grass Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jungle Dump
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Mega Gardevoir ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overflowing Wishes
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mega Symphonia
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Run Errand
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Draw 2 cards. You can't use more than 1 Run Errand Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Rapid-Fire Combo
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

name: Mega Lucario ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aura Jab
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mega Brave
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Mega Brave.

name: Acerola's Mischief
rules: You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.  Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon ex.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Lt. Surge's Bargain
rules: Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mega Lucario ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aura Jab
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mega Brave
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Mega Brave.

name: Oddish
flavorText: Its scientific name is Oddium wanderus. It is said to cover distances as far as 1,000 feet when night falls, walking on its two roots.

name: Gloom

attacks.name: Disperse Drool
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

name: Vileplume

attacks.name: Pollen Bomb
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Lively Flower
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: The bud bursts into bloom with a bang. It then starts scattering allergenic, poisonous pollen.

name: Mega Heracross ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Juggernaut Horn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

name: Lotad
flavorText: The leaf on its head is naturally dirt repellent and will stay clean even after transporting Pokémon that are covered in mud.

name: Lombre

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers waterfronts with plentiful food. It became nocturnal so it wouldn't have to compete for food with bird Pokémon.

name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Excited Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have any Grass Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Bug's Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Nymble

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

name: Lokix

attacks.name: Jumping Shot
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

name: Charmander

abilities.name: Agile
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: The flame on its tail shows the strength of its life-force. If Charmander is weak, the flame also burns weakly.

name: Charmeleon
flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, the temperature around it rises higher and higher, tormenting its opponents.

name: Mega Charizard X ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Inferno X
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fire Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Fighting Wings
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.

name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it's pushed or pulled.

name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Burning Flare
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

name: Oricorio ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Excited Turbo
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, if you have any Fire Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Fire Pokémon.

name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Ceruledge

attacks.name: Infernal Slash
attacks.text: Discard 4 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand. If you can't discard 4 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

name: Seel

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The protrusion on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.

name: Dewgong

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's colder.

name: Swinub
flavorText: It searches for food by digging into the ground with its snout. Even frozen ground doesn't give it any trouble.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Covered by a shaggy coat, it is resistant to the cold. Its tusks of ice thicken when it snows.

name: Mamoswine

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

attacks.name: Blizzard Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.

name: Suicune

attacks.name: Crystal Fall
attacks.text: If you have at least 4 Water Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Call for Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Targeted Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees in half with a single hit.

name: Rotom ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Multi Adapter
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has "Rotom" in its name may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached. If this Ability goes away, discard Pokémon Tools from those Pokémon until only 1 remains on each.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Yamper

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

name: Boltund

attacks.name: Electric Run
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

name: Pawmi

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

name: Pawmo
flavorText: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

name: Pawmot

attacks.name: Voltaic Fist
attacks.text: You may have this Pokémon also do 60 damage to itself and make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

name: Misdreavus
flavorText: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

name: Mismagius ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Swirling Prose
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Hexa-Magic
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

name: Snubbull
flavorText: In truth, it is a cowardly Pokémon. It growls eagerly in order to hide its fear from its opponent.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If attacked, it desperately flails its limbs about in an attempt to repel its opponent.

name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Swelling Light
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Psychic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Soothing Melody
attacks.text: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

name: Mega Diancie ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Diamond Coat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Garland Ray
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Mimikyu

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

name: Milcery

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The more sweet aromas it absorbs, the more its body swells. Milcery will deflate when its energy level drops.

name: Alcremie

attacks.name: Sweet Circle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Desserts that Alcremie have decorated with their cream have a rich, sweet flavor and bring happiness to all who eat them.

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

name: Bramblin

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Place 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

name: Brambleghast

abilities.name: Prison Panic
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Make your opponent's Active Pokémon Confused.

flavorText: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

name: Paldean Tauros

attacks.name: Raging Charge
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon that has "Tauros" in its name that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a muscular body and excels at close-quarters combat. It uses its short horns to strike the opponent's weak spots.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It builds its nest on a steep cliff. When it is done gliding, it hops along the ground back to its nest.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: If it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It patiently awaits prey inside its funnel- shaped nest.

name: Vibrava
flavorText: Rather than using its underdeveloped wings for flight, it rubs them together, emitting ultrasonic waves to attack its enemies.

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sandy Flapping
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

name: Gastly
flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

name: Haunter
flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

name: Mega Gengar ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shadowy Concealment
abilities.text: If 1 of your Darkness Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon ex, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. The effect of Shadowy Concealment doesn't stack.

attacks.name: Void Gale
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Sniping Feathers
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Cocky Claw
attacks.text: If you have any Stage 2 Darkness Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

name: Mega Sharpedo ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Greedy Fang
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hungry Jaws
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

name: Seviper

abilities.name: Excited Power
abilities.text: If you have any Darkness Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Because of this Pokémon's ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

name: Sandile
flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

name: Krokorok
flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Vengeful Fang
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

name: Toxel

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel's body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Sinister Surge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

name: Eternatus

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Power Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

name: Empoleon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Emperor's Stance
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Iron Feathers
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Tool Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all Pokémon.

flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Duraludon's body is comprised of a special metal that's lightweight and scratch resistant. It's also smooth, as though it was given a mirror finish.

name: Archaludon

attacks.name: Coated Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Ball Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand, and your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Dual Tail
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Energizing Sketch
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Attach an amount of Basic Energy up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Once a Smeargle reaches adulthood, it will have other members of its species leave paw prints on its back.

name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

name: Linoone

abilities.name: Excited Dash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, and if you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Run Around
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

name: Mega Lopunny ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 170 more damage.

attacks.name: Spiky Hopper
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Battle Cage

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Blowtorch
rules: You can use this card only if you discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand.  Discard a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, or discard a Stadium in play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dizzying Valley
rules: Confused Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) don't recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Firebreather
rules: Search your deck for up to 7 Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Grimsley's Move
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put a Darkness Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck. You can't use this card during your first turn.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Jumbo Ice Cream
rules: Heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon that has 3 or more Energy attached.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Punk Helmet
rules: If the Darkness Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Sacred Charm
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have an Ability (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Wondrous Patch
rules: Attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Psychic Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Excited Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have any Grass Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

name: Nymble

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

name: Dewgong

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's colder.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Call for Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

name: Yamper

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Limit Break
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sandy Flapping
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

name: Paldean Wooper

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It's dangerous for Wooper to travel alone. They line up in groups of three or four and help each other as they walk around the wetlands.

name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Sinister Surge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Dual Tail
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

name: Erika's Gloom

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It secretes a sticky, drool-like nectar. Though sweet, it smells too repulsive to get very close.

name: Erika's Vileplume ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Lovely Fragrance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bloom Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

name: Dustox

abilities.name: Boisterous Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

name: Erika's Weepinbell

attacks.name: Leafy Cyclone
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

name: Grubbin

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

name: Team Rocket's Tarountula

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.

name: Erika's Oddish

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When it is woken by moonlight, it wanders about. But during the day, it stays still underground.

attacks.name: Flower Garden Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Erika's Pokémon in play.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sun rays.

name: Mega Meganium ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giant Bouquet
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Team Rocket's Spidops

abilities.name: Charging Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

name: Erika's Bellsprout
flavorText: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

name: Erika's Tangela

abilities.name: Gathering of Blossoms
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for an Erika's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

name: Wurmple
flavorText: It loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a Starly, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

name: Silcoon

abilities.name: Multiplying Cocoon
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Silcoon or a Cascoon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Energy Straw
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and this attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there.

flavorText: Vibrantly patterned wings are its prominent feature. It sucks sweet flower nectar with its long mouth.

name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Trading Places
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body, which is made of soft silk, hardens over time. When cracks appear, evolution is near.

name: Budew

attacks.name: Itchy Pollen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

name: Charmander
flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander’s life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Charmeleon
flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

name: Ethan's Slugma
flavorText: Its lava body can cool and chip away at times, but a magma bath will heal it right up.

name: Numel

abilities.name: Incandescent Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

name: Ethan's Magcargo

abilities.name: Melt Away
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.text: Discard up to 5 Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives near the craters of volcanoes. It stores fire energy in its shell of cooled and hardened magma.

name: Entei

attacks.name: Flare Fall
attacks.text: If you have at least 4 Fire Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

name: Ethan's Ho-Oh ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Golden Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Ethan's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shining Feathers
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon.

name: Tepig
flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

name: N's Darumaka
flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

name: Mega Charizard Y ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Explosion Y
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 280 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Roasting Burn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Burned, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Power Stomp
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The volcanoes on its back have a major eruption every 10 years--or whenever it becomes really angry.

name: Pignite

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The more it eats, the more fuel it has to make the fire in its stomach stronger. This fills it with even more power.

name: Mega Emboar ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.

name: N's Darmanitan

attacks.name: Back Draft
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Basic Energy card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Flamebody Cannon
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Sudden Scorching
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from their hand. If this Pokémon evolved from Salandit during this turn, your opponent discards 2 more cards.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When two Salazzle meet, they will use their pheromone gas to fight over the males in each other’s group.

name: Psyduck

abilities.name: Damp
abilities.text: Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) lose any Ability that requires the Pokémon using it to Knock Out itself.

flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

name: Regice ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Glastrier

attacks.name: Ice Shot
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Frosty Typhoon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frosty Typhoon.

flavorText: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It’s also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

name: Pikachu ex
rules: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
